matches.x: CHAPTER VII. David desireth to build a house unto God-— God forbiddeth him—David’s prayer. ND it came to pass, when. the king sat in-his house, and. the Lord had given him rest round about from all his enemies ; :

"You knew Shabti and Kumi, etc. You knew that when I was in trouble, I did not forget you. And so it says (2 Samuel 7:1) "And it came to pass, when the king sat in his house, and the LORD had given him rest round about." Perhaps I did not tell you to build the Temple. You knew Shabti and Kumi. And when I fled after Absalom, I did not call after you in protest. Rather (Psalm 3:1) "A Psalm of David when he fled from Absalom his son." Rabbi Judah says that this Psalm was said by Adam the first man.

for them who shall be heirs of salvation The whole discussion about heirs of salvation is being established by this first chapter. Christ is the heir of the Father. Later, the discussion [see chapter 3] will move to 'entering into the rest of the Lord.' There is an ancient theme of equating rest and inherit. The Dead Sea Scrolls (4Q Florilegium) contains three references which ties them together. Also, the Septuagint translates the word rest as inherit in 2 Sam. 7:1. The KJV reads "and the LORD…
